title: Lost TV Show Found: Morecambe and Wise Come Back!
summary: A long-lost TV show from 1968 by the famous comedy duo Morecambe and Wise has been found! A person who worked in TV kept a copy, and now the BBC will show it again. The show is funny and fast, with jokes and sketches like a balloon dancer and a nudist camp. Professor Justin Smith, who helped find the show, says it shows the duo at their best. The show was made when they returned to the BBC after working on other TV. Even though the found copy is in black and white, it can be fixed to look better. Fans are excited to see this lost episode again. The BBC plans to broadcast it in the next few months.

keywords.explanation: Two people who work together to make people laugh
One part of a TV show
To show on TV or radio
Something kept by a person, not a museum or library
Short funny scenes in a show
To make something old look like new again

questions.question: Who are the famous comedy duo in the article?
When was the lost TV show first broadcast?
Where was the copy of the show found?
What color is the found recording?
What does the BBC plan to do with the show?

background_read: Morecambe and Wise were a very popular comedy duo in the UK from the 1960s to 1980s. They had a TV show that millions watched. Sometimes old TV shows get lost because they were not saved properly. Finding a lost show is like finding treasure for fans.
Article_Structure: WHO: Morecambe and Wise, the comedy duo, and Professor Justin Smith who helped find the show.
WHAT: A lost TV episode from 1968 was found and will be broadcast again.
WHERE: The show was found in a private collection in the UK.
WHY: The show was lost because old TV shows were not always saved, but now it can be enjoyed again.

perspectives.description: They are very happy to see a lost show from their favorite duo.
They think finding lost shows is important for history.
